name: aggregator handles operator file with both overrides AND new rules in same file
command: bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/rubric/aggregator.sh" --root "$PWD/tree" --format json | node -e 'let s="";process.stdin.on("data",d=>s+=d);process.stdin.on("end",()=>{const r=JSON.parse(s);const newOp=r.rules.find(x=>x.id==="acme-100");const overridingOp=r.rules.filter(x=>x.id==="g-rule"&&x.origin==="operator")[0];const supersededPlugin=r.rules.filter(x=>x.id==="g-rule"&&x.origin==="plugin")[0];const failures=[];if(!newOp||newOp.origin!=="operator")failures.push("missing or wrong origin for new operator rule acme-100");if(newOp&&(newOp.superseded_by_operator||newOp.supersedes))failures.push("new acme-100 should have no supersedes markers");if(!overridingOp)failures.push("missing operator-override entry for g-rule");if(!supersededPlugin||supersededPlugin.superseded_by_operator!==true)failures.push("plugin g-rule not marked superseded");if(failures.length===0){process.stderr.write("ok\n")}else{process.stderr.write("FAIL: "+failures.join("; ")+"\n");process.exit(1)}});'
setup:
cat > tree/google/g.yaml << 'EOF'
source:
  id: g
  authoritative_publisher: Google
  authoritative_url: https://example.com/g
  registry_link: STANDARDS-URLS.yaml
  fetched_at: 2026-05-09T08:00:00Z
  content_hash: sha256:plugin
rules:
  - id: g-rule
    name: g
    description: plugin g rule.
    detector: rubric/detectors/g.sh
recommended_set: [g-rule]
all_set: [g-rule]
EOF

cat > tree/_operator/google/extensions.yaml << 'EOF'
source:
  id: g
  authoritative_publisher: Google (operator extension)
  authoritative_url: https://example.com/g
  registry_link: STANDARDS-URLS.yaml
  fetched_at: 2026-05-09T08:00:00Z
  content_hash: sha256:operator
rules:
  - id: g-rule
    name: g
    description: operator-overridden g rule.
    detector: rubric/detectors/g.sh
  - id: acme-100
    name: acme-extra
    description: brand new operator rule (no plugin counterpart).
    detector: .claude-tdd-pro/detectors/acme-extra.sh
recommended_set: [g-rule, acme-100]
all_set: [g-rule, acme-100]
EOF
